news.status: Under a joint Iowa DOT / Illinois DOT corridor study. As of May 2026 the two options still on the table would both demolish and replace the 86-year-old bridge; a decision is expected when the study wraps in 2027.

Iowa City approved an initial concept to replace this 1915 span (the eastbound Burlington Street Bridge); construction is projected to begin around 2029. It was flagged structurally deficient in a 2021 inspection report.

Closed since April 6, 2026 for a ~120-day rehabilitation (finger-joint and deck-overlay work, bearing replacement, railing work) funded partly by a $1.5M federal grant. City expects it open again during the 2026 construction season; RAGBRAI passed through while it was still closed.

Under active reconstruction since March 17, 2026: full bridge deck, barrier rail, sidewalk and light-pole replacement, plus a new 10-ft bike trail. City expects it done by mid-October 2026; it's meant to serve as the main downtown crossing once the Gordon Drive viaduct is later demolished.

No longer the same bridge: the 113-year-old span was demolished in 2024 and fully replaced. The City of Des Moines' own project page lists the $19.1M replacement as completed in spring 2026, with new bike lanes and wider sidewalks.

Iowa DOT is proposing a full replacement at this Mapleton junction (longer, wider bridge) and was collecting public feedback as of Feb 2026. Construction isn't expected to start until spring 2028, with completion later that year — so it stays open and posted for a couple more construction seasons yet.
Open but weight-posted at 37 tons after emergency repairs in 2025. The city has scheduled a full re-decking for 2027, with additional interim deck repairs expected to keep it serviceable until then.

Replaced. Greene County awarded the replacement contract in July 2024, and the new bridge deck was being poured by the end of March 2025 — this one should come off the chronically-Poor list on the next NBI cycle.
